hypothesis: If metal superhydride discovery is structured around chemical template theory focusing on interstitial electron localization (quasi-atoms) in metal lattices as a primary screening criterion combined with machine learning-predicted thermodynamic stability, then this workflow will more efficiently identify novel stable and high-temperature superconducting metal superhydrides compared to unguided structural searches, because constraining the chemical search space using physically meaningful descriptors reduces the computational cost while preserving the most promising candidate structures.
